channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government pledges firm action against xenophobic violence following diplomatic tensions with Ghana
item.description: South African authorities have committed to taking decisive action against xenophobic attacks targeting foreign nationals, particularly Ghanaians, following diplomatic concerns raised by Ghana. The mo...

item.title: Rain or Shine defeats Magnolia in a tense fourth quarter showdown
item.description: In a thrilling PBA match, the Rain or Shine Elasto Painters managed to overcome the Magnolia Hotshots with a strong performance in the fourth quarter. The game, which saw heightened tensions between t...

item.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Concludes Meetings with Pakistani Leaders on Regional Ceasefire
item.description: An Iranian delegation headed by Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi completed a visit to Islamabad, where he met Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of Defence Forces, Field Marshal Asim Munir. During...

item.title: Eala and Sönmez bow out of Madrid Open doubles after three-set battle
item.description: Filipina tennis standout Alex Eala and her partner Zeynep Sönmez saw their campaign in the Madrid Open women’s doubles come to an end after a hard-fought match marked by a strong second-set recovery. ...

item.title: Former Police Officer Speaks Out on Mental Health Crisis in Australian Policing
item.description: A former police officer has penned a powerful opinion piece in The Canberra Times, detailing the escalating mental health crisis within Australian policing. The officer, whose name has been withheld f...

item.title: Pakistan Successfully Launches Indigenous EO-3 Satellite with Chinese Support
item.description: In a significant achievement for Pakistan’s space ambitions, Suparco has successfully launched the country’s indigenous Electro-Optical Satellite, EO-3, from the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Centre in Chi...

item.title: Military Officers Face Court-Martial Under Harsh Conditions, Families Barred from Attendance
item.description: For now, 36 military officers dey face trial for alleged coup-related offences for Abuja under General Court Martial wey Defence Headquarters (DHQ) set up. The officers show for court with leg chains ...

item.title: Delhi Business School Chairman Discusses Importance of Skills vs Academic Marks
item.description: The article focuses on a significant discussion led by the chairman of a reputed business school in Delhi, addressing the age-old debate of academic marks versus practical skills. According to the cha...

item.title: Conrad Black: Trump's Strategy for Addressing the Iran Conflict
item.description: According to an opinion piece by Conrad Black, the ongoing conflict with Iran is currently in a state of artificial stasis, fueled by a widespread misconception that Iran is 'winning' due to its survi...

item.title: RAF jets deployed from Romania to monitor Russian drones near NATO border without engagement
item.description: Two RAF Eurofighter Typhoon jets were launched from a Romanian air base in the early hours of Saturday after Russian drones were detected operating close to NATO airspace near Ukraine. The aircraft es...

item.title: Michael Jackson Biopic Sparks Online Debate on Legacy and Accountability
item.description: The new Michael Jackson biopic 'Michael,' directed by Antoine Fuqua and starring Jaafar Jackson, opened on April 24, 2026, projecting $70 million in its U.S. debut, marking the largest opening for a m...

item.title: PCSO Lotto Draw Results for April 25, 2026
item.description: The article reports the official lotto results for April 25, 2026, as released by authorized sources in the Philippines. These results are part of the regular public service updates provided to inform...

item.title: AAP MPs shifting to BJP stir political tensions ahead of Punjab 2027 elections
item.description: A significant political development has unfolded in Punjab with seven out of ten Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) Rajya Sabha MPs, including Raghav Chadha, switching to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). This mov...

item.title: Ontario Couple Found Guilty Over Alleged 'Kill List' Plot
item.description: A couple in Ontario has been convicted in connection with a disturbing case involving a 'kill list' manifesto. The case, which drew national attention due to its alarming nature, concluded with both i...

item.title: Exploring the Garden Route: Must-See Stops Between Wilderness and Sedgefield
item.description: The Garden Route in South Africa has been named the best road trip in the world, surpassing famous routes like Italy's Amalfi Coast and America's Route 66. Stretching roughly 300km from Mossel Bay to ...

item.title: President Zardari’s China visit to strengthen economic ties and advance CPEC cooperation
item.description: President Asif Ali Zardari has departed for an official visit to China, where his engagements will mainly focus on boosting economic and trade cooperation, along with further progress on the China-Pak...

item.title: Presidency Responds To ADC's Criticism Over Tinubu’s 'Noise' Comment
item.description: The Presidency has responded to the criticism from the African Democratic Congress (ADC) following President Bola Tinubu's remarks about their national convention. Sunday Dare, the Special Adviser to ...

item.title: India plans to host the 2038 Asian Games in Ahmedabad
item.description: India is planning to bid to host the 2038 Asian Games in Ahmedabad, the head of the Indian Olympic Association (IOA) confirmed. This move is part of India's ongoing efforts to bring major internationa...

item.title: Tanzania’s Hip-Hop Scene Revives Through Freestyle Battles
item.description: Over the past few weeks, Tanzania’s hip-hop culture has seen a fresh surge thanks to freestyle rap battles dominating social media. Unlike the mainstream Bongo Flava genre, this trend emphasizes spont...

item.title: Differences in Women’s Roles Between the Anglican and Catholic Churches
item.description: This report dey compare how Anglican Church and Catholic Church dey treat women, especially as Sarah Mullally, the first woman wey lead Church of England, dey visit Rome to meet Pope Leo XIV. The meet...
